name: Deep-Fried Plantain Rounds
keywords: big flavors of the hot sun
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure you use green plantains; they are slightly harder to peel but will hold their shape better during frying. Ripe plantains may become too mushy for this preparation.
2. Peel the plantains and cut them into 2-inch rounds.
3. Heat the oil in a small saucepan until hot but not smoking.
4. Carefully drop the plantain rounds into the hot oil, about 3 or 4 at a time. Cook each batch for 2 to 3 minutes or until well browned on all sides.
5. Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried plantains from the oil and let them drain on paper towels.
6. Flatten each plantain by pressing it gently with a rolling pin or frying pan until it is circular in shape.
7. Return the flattened plantain sections to the hot oil, again about 3 or 4 at a time. Cook for an additional 2 minutes or until the entire surface is golden brown.
8. Remove the fried plantains from the oil using a slotted spoon and let them drain on paper towels once more.
9. Season generously with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Serve immediately hot or allow to cool to room temperature before serving.
